summary: All windows flash white when first opened

description.text:
### Description

When opening the app or any popup windows (e.g. preferences, about, check for updates), the window briefly flashes bright white before showing its contents. I need dark mode for accessibility reasons, and the white flash makes the app extremely difficult to use.

Note: the white flash is most noticeable in dark mode, but happens with light mode as well. 

### Reproducing

Open the app or any window that's part of the app (e.g. Preferences, About, Check for updates).

### Zettlr version

3.5.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Xubuntu 20.04 and 22.04

### Additional Information

This same white flash also occurs in other Electron apps - there's an example in this article: [Electron white screen app startup](https://www.christianengvall.se/electron-white-screen-app-startup/). The suggested fix is to either set a background colour when a new BrowserWindow is drawn, or to use a 'ready to show' event to only show the window after the contents are drawn. There's a detailed explanation of how to do this in the [BrowserWindow page of the Electron documentation](https://www.electronjs.org/docs/latest/api/browser-window/#showing-window-gracefully).

### Description

The bug described in #570 is still there.

### Reproducing

Switch on dark mode, close the app and reopen it.

### Zettlr version

3.5.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS

### Additional Information

This has also been reported by @CodexEnt and @dm5n and was also present with Zettlr 3.4.4 on Windows

summary: Quarto cross-referencing system for LATEX equations breaks UI text formatting

description.text:
### Description

Zettlr supports Quarto (.qmd) files. The latter have a cross-referencing syntax for figures, tables and equations based on this structure `{#eq-anid}`, see [Quarto doc](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/cross-references.html) for more details:

```qmd
$$
E=mc^2
$${#eq-id}
```

When opened in Zettlr, a .qmd file with this structure seems to break the UI, see the screenshot below.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/009857a1-d279-4240-9044-21b277d15110)

I expect Zettlr to display the formula as normal with the `{#eq-id}` on its side.

Note: the use of [Pandoc's divs](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/cross-references-divs.html) does not work for equations. 

This is related to https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/4380 yet not totally similar as it focuses on the display of the qmd file in the Zettlr UI.

### Reproducing

Open a markdown file with extension .qmd and copy-paste the following:

```qmd
## A title

This is an equation:

$$
E=mc^2
$${#eq-id}

## Another title
```

### Zettlr version

3.5.1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

### Description

Zettlr opens an auto update dialogue box with the message "Cannot Check for Update" The error message is: There was an error while checking for updates. EPROTO: Could not check for updates. RequestError: write EPROTO 1116692703648:error:100000f7:SSL routines:OPENSSL_internal:WRONG_VERSION_NUMBER:../../third_party/boringssl/src/ssl/tls_record.cc:130:

### Reproducing

Open Zettlr and leave it running for a while until it checks for updates.

### Zettlr version

Version 3.5.0 (3.5.0)

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

MacOs Sequoia 15.5

### Additional Information

This does not stop the app from functioning; just thought I'd let the devs know.

summary: Crash on loading workspace including large .txt file

description.text:
### Description

I was having issues opening my notes database, which is about 1000 files (including attachments) and is about 180 MB.
 I narrowed this issue down to a sub-sub-subdirectory, containing 25 files.

Ultimately, by splitting the files up by type, I was able to narrow it down to one specific text file, which is a 39 MB text dump of linux `journalctl`. It's about 40 million lines, which is obviously large, but just having that in a subfolder of the directory that Zettlr is attempting to open as a workspace appears to be enough to crash the program. Clearing the FSAL cache does nothing, and nothing is written to the log after the Zettlr startup messages.

Is this a known issue? I'm not sure why Zettlr is attempting to parse all the component non-Markdown text files just by opening the folder as a workspace. It would make a lot of sense if large _markdown_ files caused issues, but this is a `.txt` file. Is there a way to tell Zettlr to ignore specific files (or ideally, tell it to ignore .txt files entirely) for whatever kind of fulltext loading it's doing when opening a workspace? I did check the preferences, but I didn't see anything.

I did my due diligence checking for duplicates, but apologies if I missed one.

### Reproducing

Open any directory with a large text file in it in Zettlr. I used the following (unix) commands to generate an example:
```bash
mkdir -p testdir
cd testdir
tr -dc A-Za-z0-9 </dev/urandom | head -c 40000000 > test.txt # generate a ~40MB text file
```
...then open the `testdir` in Zettlr.

### Zettlr version

3.4.2, 3.4.4, 3.5.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 24H2

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Fix: Debounce scroll to prevent flickering on input

description.text:
## Description

This pull request implements a debounce mechanism to prevent unnecessary scroll flickering during rapid input events in the editor. The fix improves user experience by ensuring smoother text input and cursor visibility without sudden jumps.

## Changes

- Added a `scrollTimer` and `setTimeout` logic to debounce scroll adjustments in the `EditorView.updateListener`.
- Ensured the scroll only triggers when the cursor selection is out of the visible viewport.
- Applied a 50ms delay to avoid excessive updates during continuous typing.

No breaking API changes introduced.

## Additional information

- Tested on Windows 11 (x64) using latest stable Zettlr `develop` branch.
- Verified that the scroll behavior now performs consistently without flickering in distraction-free mode, typewriter mode, and default editor.
- Ran `yarn lint` and ensured code style matches the repository.
- Confirmed `CHANGELOG.md` was updated with appropriate entry.
- Fixes #5732


<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: Windows 11, Visual Studio Code, Node.js v18.x, Yarn v1.x

summary: [Draft] Add Zotero citation picker support with mode toggle

description.text:
## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
We have successfully integrated the Zotero citation picker into Zettlr using Zotero’s Better BibTeX CAYW HTTP protocol. Users can now choose between two citation modes in the Preferences panel: Original, which uses Zettlr’s local citation database, and Zotero, which opens the native Zotero picker and inserts citekeys dynamically. This is fully integrated with the autocomplete system and wired into the editor using CodeMirror’s reactive state mechanism.

Note: This implementation is a partial step toward [#1970](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/1970), but remains a work in progress. Zotero citekeys are not yet rendered via the citeproc renderer, so they only display correctly if the corresponding Zotero library is also included as a citation file in Zettlr. Additionally, the selected citation mode is not persisted across restarts as it only takes effect after manually switching modes during a session.

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
The change allows the selection of two modes:
Zotero Mode: Real-time integration with Zotero for dynamic citation retrieval
Original Mode: Static database-driven citations with usage-based sorting
When no configuration is present it defaults to 'original' mode. It uses CodeMirror's StateEffect/StateField pattern for reactive updates and citation mode changes are dispatched through CodeMirror's effect system. Then it integrates with the main configuration system. The EditorConfiguration interface includes citationMode and it is set to 'original' in the default configuration. Any configuration updates trigger appropriate state field updates

source/common/services/zoteroService.ts
→ Added new service to fetch citations from Zotero via the Better BibTeX CAYW endpoint.
→ Registers IPC handler 'zotero:search'.

source/main.ts
→ Imports and registers ZoteroService on app.whenReady.

source/common/modules/markdown-editor/autocomplete/citations.ts
→ Added citationMode as a StateField and StateEffect.
→ Autocomplete entries() dynamically switch between Zotero and original mode.
→ Refactored apply function for citeStyle compatibility.

source/common/modules/markdown-editor/util/configuration.ts
→ Extended EditorConfiguration with citationMode.
→ Set default to 'original'.

source/win-preferences/schema/citations.ts
→ Added UI radio toggle for switching between Zotero and Original citation engines.

source/common/modules/markdown-editor/index.ts
→ Dispatches citationMode on editor initialization.

source/win-main/MainEditor.vue
→ Dispatches citationMode effect into the editor.
→ Watches config changes and updates citation mode reactively.

source/app/service-providers/config/get-config-template.ts
→ Added citationMode to configuration template.

source/app/service-providers/commands/force-open.ts
→ Minor comment cleanup.

forge.config.js
→ Removed redundant logic for downloading/bundling Pandoc across platforms. 

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->
 
<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS (noble) via WSL2 on Windows 11
 Tested and working

summary: feat(graph): Add folder filter dropdown to restrict graph view

description.text:
Implements: [#5584](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/5584) — Restrict Graph View by Workspace

What’s New?
A dropdown UI is added to the Graph View toolbar to select a folder from the current workspace.

Selecting a folder will filter the graph to show only nodes and connections from that directory.

Selecting "All folders" resets the graph to its original state (showing all files).

The view updates reactively — any folder change triggers a rebuild of the graph via the buildGraph() function.

Why is this useful?
As described in the issue, users working on multiple writing projects or using Zettlr for Zettelkasten-style note-taking often organise notes into folders. This feature enables users to:

Focus the graph on just one writing project or context.
Reduce visual noise from unrelated notes.
Maintain better clarity across diverse folders within the workspace.

UI Design Reference:
The feature follows the UX pattern of the Global Search > Restrict to directory dropdown for consistency and ease of use.

Caveats:
This implementation focuses strictly on folder-based filtering. Future enhancements like filtering by search results or multi-select items in the file tree can be considered as separate features.

### Description

If you want to use the RETURN/ENTER-Key in the Asset-Manager (tabs "Export", "Import", "Custom CSS" and "Snippets") to insert a new line, nothing happens.

However, "Copy/Paste" from Clipboard works.

Expected: Using the ENTER-Key in the respective tab (like "Custom CSS") creates a new line at the cursor position.

### Reproducing

Open Zettlr > Datei > Einstellungen > Asset-Manager > go to "Custom CSS" > position cursor at any position > try to enter a new line in by pressing ENTER

### Zettlr version

3.5.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub


### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Operating System Version

Win10

### Additional Information

That issue does not occur with older versions (like 3.4.4 or 3.4.2)

summary: Vim editor, in normal mode 's' does not work as expected

description.text:
### Description

When using the Vim input mode, and in normal mode, pressing `s` should function the same as pressing `cl`. It does not. Instead of deleting the character under the cursor and changing to insert mode. It inserts an "s" and remains in normal mode.

### Reproducing

1. In Preferences change the input mode to Vim.
2. In the editor, with the cursor in the middle of a string of characters, press `s`.

### Zettlr version

3.5.0

### Installation Method

summary: Fix: Related files list disappearing when switching sidebar tabs

description.text:
Fixes #5789 - Related files list disappears when switching tabs in the sidebar.

<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.
  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:
  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues
  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
Fixes #5789 - The related files list disappears when switching tabs in the sidebar. Changed `v-if` to `v-show` for sidebar tab components to prevent component destruction and maintain state.

## Changes
Changed `v-if` to `v-show` for all sidebar tab components in `MainSidebar.vue`:
- `ToCTab`: `v-if="currentTab === 'toc'"` → `v-show="currentTab === 'toc'"`
- `ReferencesTab`: `v-if="currentTab === 'references'"` → `v-show="currentTab === 'references'"`
- `RelatedFilesTab`: `v-if="currentTab === 'relatedFiles'"` → `v-show="currentTab === 'relatedFiles'"`
- `OtherFilesTab`: `v-if="currentTab === 'attachments'"` → `v-show="currentTab === 'attachments'"`

**Technical Details**: `v-if` completely destroys and recreates components when switching tabs, causing the `RelatedFilesTab` component to lose its computed state. Using `v-show` preserves component instances and maintains state.

No breaking API changes.

## Additional information
**Testing**:
- Created test files with shared tags and internal links
- Confirmed Related Files tab displays the related files list correctly
- Switched between different sidebar tabs multiple times
- Verified the related files list persists and doesn't disappear

**Impact**: Only affects sidebar tab switching behavior, improves user experience by maintaining state consistency.

**Checklist Notes**: Items 4 and 5 (yarn lint/test and CHANGELOG.md) - need guidance on these requirements.

Tested on: Windows 11
